front: Period: signed the Treaty of Zgorzelec with Poland, recognizing the Oder-Neisse line as its eastern border

front: Period: its legislature passed its first-ever non-unanimous resolution in a 1972 vote, legalizing abortion

front: Period: the term "Republikflucht" ("flight from the republic") described widespread emigration from the country in its early history

front: Institution: Walter Ulbricht's 1963 policy that replaced the initial Five-Year Plans and pursued a strategy of "overtaking without catching up"
work: Government, the SED, and Walter Ulbricht's Leadership

front: Figure: led East Germany as First Secretary of the SED from 1950 to 1971, developing the New Economic System
work: Government, the SED, and Walter Ulbricht's Leadership

front: Figure: East Germany's last leader, who resigned after the fall of the Berlin Wall
work: Government, the SED, and Walter Ulbricht's Leadership

front: Event: built in 1961 to seal off West Berlin, officially termed the "Anti-Fascist Protective Wall"
work: The Berlin Wall & Division from West Germany

front: Event: signed by East Germany and West Germany to loosen border restrictions, shortly before the Basic Treaty
work: The Berlin Wall & Division from West Germany

front: Event: a construction workers' strike against a 10% quota increase under the "New Course" program spread nationwide, with workers marching onto Stalin Allee
Event: protesters used the slogans "we don't want to be slaves anymore" and "down with the government, down with the police," aided by the underground Christian organization Young Congregation
Event: the Bitterfeld committee issued nine demands during this uprising, the most influential of the workers' councils that emerged
Event: Soviet tanks were deployed to crush this uprising, and the People's Police (VoPo) fired on civilians
Event: the U.S. sent food-aid boxes of peas, flour, milk, and lard, nicknamed for this American president, to citizens affected by the uprising
Event: June 17 was later commemorated as a public holiday, "Day of German Unity," in West Germany in memory of this uprising
Event: protestors disparaged three leaders with the slogan "A goatee beard, a paunch and spectacles are not what the people want!"
Figure: was stripped of power as Soviet security chief following the workers' strike that sparked the 1953 East German uprising
Work: Gunter Grass's play about actors rehearsing a production during the actual 1953 East German uprising
back: The Plebeians Rehearse the Uprising (Gunter Grass)

front: Institution: East Germany's secret police, which employed as many as one in seven citizens as informants
Figure: East Germany's foreign-intelligence spymaster, dubbed "the man without a face," who pioneered the use of seductive "Romeo" agents
Institution: East Germany's state-sponsored athletic doping program, organized by SED member Manfred Ewald at the SV Dynamo sports club using drugs like Oral-Turinabol
Institution: replaced Catholic confirmation after the Central Committee weakened the clergy's influence; involved an oath to the state taken at age 14
Period: the country's signature automotive product was this cheaply-made plastic car with an obsolete two-stroke engine, for which citizens sometimes waited a decade
Institution: stores that accepted only the country's own "Forum checks" (introduced 1979) or foreign currency
Period: phased out this Nylon-6 fabric, used in mesh-net shopping bags, during the 1970s oil crisis
Event: sparked by a Brazilian "black frost" that wrecked that country's harvest, this 1977 crisis led East Germany to invest in Vietnamese and Laotian coffee production
Work: a fashion magazine founded in 1956 by an East German photographer, offering weekly sewing tips
Period: nicknamed the "little light man," this walking pedestrian-signal figure in a straw hat was designed based on photographs of the country's leaders
Event: Walter Ulbricht's 1958 proclamation of "Ten Commandments" for a socialist "New Man" preceded a police crackdown on this beach subculture
back: Free body culture (FKK) / nudist beach culture (East Germany)

front: Period: many senior officials lived on an elliptical street named for this Soviet poet
Period: the country's own Coca-Cola knock-off, sought after by the protagonist of the film Goodbye, Lenin!
Institution: children joined this group, named for a German Communist Party leader, before graduating to the FDJ
work: Youth Culture & Organizations Under the SED

front: Institution: East Germany's state youth organization, which the Thalmann Pioneers graduated into
work: Youth Culture & Organizations Under the SED

front: Event: a 1965 Leipzig riot against Erich Honecker's ban on most rock music, sparked partly by Beatles concerts across the border, that ended Walter Ulbricht's cautious youth-culture liberalization
work: Youth Culture & Organizations Under the SED

front: Period: youth attended these informal rock concerts held in churches
work: Youth Culture & Organizations Under the SED

front: Period: in the 1970s, the state supplied youth with this coveted clothing item through brands like Shanty and Wisent
work: Youth Culture & Organizations Under the SED

front: Institution: West Germany's policy that threatened to sever relations with any country recognizing East Germany
work: Foreign Relations: Isolation, Ostpolitik, and African Alliances

front: Institution: Willy Brandt's policy that replaced the Hallstein Doctrine and formally recognized East Germany as sovereign in the Basic Treaty
work: Foreign Relations: Isolation, Ostpolitik, and African Alliances

front: Institution: East Germany's Free German Youth sent these groups to train workers in several African nations
work: Foreign Relations: Isolation, Ostpolitik, and African Alliances

front: Period: Guinea's first-ever international treaty allowed East Germany to establish this in Conakry
work: Foreign Relations: Isolation, Ostpolitik, and African Alliances

front: Period: was the subject of historian Jeffrey Herf's book on its "undeclared wars" against Israel, which argued it distinguished between "moderate" and "extremist" Arab terrorists

front: Period: rallying under the slogan "We are the people," Monday demonstrations at Leipzig's St. Nicholas Church and the Alexanderplatz demonstrations helped bring down the government in 1989
Event: political scientist Susanne Lohmann studied the role of "informational cascades" in this 1989 wave of protests
Period: protesters cut the hammer-and-wheat-wreath emblem out of this element during 1989 demonstrations against the ruling SED
Period: hundreds of the country's citizens took refuge in a foreign embassy in this city in the late 1980s
Work: Christa Wolf's novel depicting life under Stasi surveillance
Work: revelations of Christa Wolf's own cooperation with the Stasi later inflamed this public scandal
Work: Christa Wolf's novel in which the cursed narrator watches Eumelos manipulate Priam into launching the Trojan War, largely an allegory for life in East Germany
Work: a teenager dates a married, middle-aged writer as East Germany collapses in this 2021 novel
Work: protagonist Alec Leamas is shot by East Germany's secret police in this John le Carre novel
back: The Spy Who Came In From The Cold (John le Carre)

front: Work: the Disney film that fictionalized a real family's escape from East Germany via improvised hot-air balloon
